Steps:
1. Super admin logs in
2. Go to admin dashboard
3. Click Create Organization
4. Fill: name, domain, settings
5. Save
Expected Result:
Organization created:
- Record in organisations table
- org_id generated
- Default settings applied

Steps:
1. Try to create org without name
2. Submit form
Expected Result: Validation error: Name required

Steps:
1. Create org with domain example.com
2. Try to create another with same domain
Expected Result: Error: Domain already exists

Steps:
1. Open existing org
2. Edit name/domain/settings
3. Save
Expected Result:
Changes saved:
- Updated in database
- Reflected in UI

Steps:
1. Select organization
2. Click Delete
3. Confirm deletion
Expected Result:
Warning modal appears:
- Shows count of users/data
- Requires confirmation
- Only super admin can delete

Steps:
1. Admin logs in
2. Go to team management
3. Click Create Team
4. Fill: name, description
5. Save
Expected Result:
Team created:
- Record in teams table
- Linked to org_id
- No members yet

Steps:
1. Try to create team without name
2. Submit
Expected Result: Validation error: Name required

Steps:
1. Create Team A
2. Create Team B
3. View team list
Expected Result:
Both teams visible:
- Separate team_id
- Both under same org

Steps:
1. Select team
2. Edit name/description
3. Save
Expected Result: Changes saved and reflected

Steps:
1. Select team with no members
2. Click Delete
3. Confirm
Expected Result:
Team deleted:
- Removed from database
- No longer in team list

Steps:
1. Select team with members
2. Try to delete
Expected Result:
Error: Cannot delete team with members
Prompt: Remove members first

Steps:
1. Select user
2. Click Assign Team
3. Choose team
4. Save
Expected Result:
User assigned:
- team_id updated in users table
- User sees team name in profile
- Can access team data

Test Case: User can only be in one team
Steps:
1. User already in Team A
2. Assign to Team B
Expected Result:
User moved to Team B:
- team_id updated
- No longer in Team A

Steps:
1. Select user in team
2. Click Remove from Team
3. Confirm
Expected Result:
User removed:
- team_id set to null
- Still in organization
- No team access

Steps:
1. Select team
2. Choose user as team lead
3. Save
Expected Result:
Team lead assigned:
- User has team_lead role
- Can manage team members
- Sees team data

Test Case: Team lead can view team members
Steps:
1. Login as team lead
2. View team dashboard
Expected Result: Can see all team members

Test Case: Team lead can assign users to team
Steps:
1. Login as team lead
2. Add user to team
3. Save
Expected Result: User added to team

Steps:
1. Login as team lead
2. Try to delete team
Expected Result: Error: Only admin can delete

Test Case: User only sees own team's data
Steps:
1. User in Team A logs in
2. View leads/opportunities
3. Try to access Team B data
Expected Result:
Only Team A data visible:
- Leads filtered by team_id
- Opportunities filtered
- Cannot access Team B

Steps:
1. Admin logs in
2. View leads/opportunities
Expected Result:
All teams' data visible:
- Can filter by team
- Can view across org

Steps:
1. Admin toggles crm_enabled
2. Save
3. User tries to access CRM
Expected Result:
If enabled: access granted
If disabled: redirected to Sales OS

Steps:
1. Admin sets max_users = 5
2. Save
3. Try to add 6th user
Expected Result:
6th user blocked:
Error: User limit reached
Prompt to upgrade plan

Steps:
1. Admin clicks Generate Invite Code
2. Copy code
3. Share with new user
Expected Result:
Code generated:
- Unique code created
- Links to org_id
- Can be used for signup

Steps:
1. Create invite code with expiry
2. Wait until expired
3. Try to use code
Expected Result:
Code rejected:
Error: Invite code expired
User cannot register

Steps:
1. Super admin logs in
2. View org list
3. Switch to different org
Expected Result:
Switched successfully:
- Dashboard shows new org data
- Users/teams for that org
- Isolated from other orgs
